questions.question: What does cosine similarity measure between two vectors?
questions.questionZh: 余弦相似度 (Cosine Similarity) 衡量的是两个向量 (Vectors) 之间的什么？
options: The Euclidean distance between them
The angle between them, ignoring their magnitudes
The sum of their element-wise products
The maximum difference in any single dimension
optionsZh: 它们之间的欧几里得距离 (Euclidean distance)
它们之间的夹角（忽略其模长）
它们的逐元素乘积 (element-wise product) 之和
任意单一维度上的最大差值
questions.explanation: Cosine similarity measures the cosine of the angle between two vectors, normalized by their magnitudes. It ranges from -1 (opposite) to +1 (same direction) and ignores vector length, which is why it dominates in NLP where document length is noise.
questions.explanationZh: 余弦相似度（Cosine Similarity）通过计算两个向量夹角的余弦值来衡量相似性，并根据向量的模长进行归一化。其取值范围为 -1（方向相反）至 +1（方向相同）。由于该指标忽略了向量长度，因此在自然语言处理（Natural Language Processing, NLP）领域占据主导地位，因为在该领域中，文档长度通常被视为噪声。

questions.question: Why is L1 distance called 'Manhattan distance'?
questions.questionZh: 为什么L1距离（L1 Distance）被称为“曼哈顿距离”（Manhattan Distance）？
options: It was invented in Manhattan
It measures distance along a grid, as if walking on city blocks
It only works in exactly two dimensions
It computes the maximum difference across all dimensions
optionsZh: 它是在曼哈顿发明的。
它沿网格度量距离，就像在城市街区中步行一样。
它仅适用于严格的二维（two dimensions）空间。
它计算所有维度 (dimensions) 上的最大差值。
questions.explanation: L1 distance sums the absolute differences along each axis, which corresponds to the shortest path on a grid where you can only move along axes (no diagonals) — like walking on a city street grid.
questions.explanationZh: L1距离（L1 distance）对各坐标轴上的绝对差值求和，它对应于网格上的最短路径，在该网格中只能沿坐标轴移动（不可走对角线）——就像在城市街道网格中行走一样。

questions.question: Why does L1 regularization (Lasso) produce sparse weights while L2 regularization (Ridge) does not?
questions.questionZh: 为什么L1正则化（L1 Regularization，即Lasso）会产生稀疏权重（Sparse Weights），而L2正则化（L2 Regularization，即Ridge）却不会？
options: L1 uses a larger penalty constant than L2
The L1 diamond constraint has corners on the axes where weights are zero; loss contours are likely to touch at corners
L2 regularization only applies to the bias terms
L1 regularization clips weights below a threshold to zero
optionsZh: L1 使用的惩罚系数 (penalty constant) 比 L2 更大。
L1 菱形约束（L1 diamond constraint）在坐标轴上存在权重为零的角点；损失等高线（loss contours）很可能在这些角点处与之相切。
L2 正则化 (L2 Regularization) 仅适用于偏置项 (Bias Terms)。
L1 正则化 (L1 Regularization) 会将低于阈值的权重直接置为零。
questions.explanation: The L1 constraint region is a diamond shape with corners aligned with the axes. The loss function's contour ellipses are most likely to first touch the diamond at a corner, where one or more weights are exactly zero. The L2 constraint is a circle with no corners, so the touch point has all weights nonzero.
questions.explanationZh: L1约束区域 (L1 constraint region) 呈菱形，其顶点与坐标轴对齐。损失函数 (loss function) 的等高线椭圆 (contour ellipses) 最有可能首先在菱形的顶点处与之接触，此时一个或多个权重 (weights) 恰好为零。L2约束 (L2 constraint) 区域为圆形且无顶点，因此接触点处的所有权重均不为零。

questions.question: What advantage does Wasserstein distance have over KL divergence when comparing probability distributions?
questions.questionZh: 在比较概率分布（probability distributions）时，沃瑟斯坦距离（Wasserstein distance）相较于KL散度（KL divergence）具有哪些优势？
options: Wasserstein is always smaller than KL divergence
Wasserstein provides meaningful gradients even when distributions do not overlap, while KL goes to infinity
Wasserstein is symmetric but KL is not
Both B and C are correct
optionsZh: Wasserstein距离 (Wasserstein distance) 始终小于 KL散度 (Kullback-Leibler divergence)。
即使分布之间没有重叠，Wasserstein 距离（Wasserstein distance）仍能提供有意义的梯度，而 KL 散度（KL divergence）则会趋于无穷大。
Wasserstein 距离（Wasserstein distance）是对称的，但 KL 散度（Kullback-Leibler divergence）不是。
B 和 C 均正确
questions.explanation: Wasserstein distance is a true metric (symmetric, satisfies triangle inequality) and provides gradients even for non-overlapping distributions. KL divergence is asymmetric and goes to infinity when distributions don't overlap, which is why WGANs replaced KL-based GANs.
questions.explanationZh: 瓦瑟斯坦距离（Wasserstein distance）是一种严格的度量（metric）（具有对称性且满足三角不等式），即使面对不重叠的分布也能提供梯度。KL散度（KL divergence）是非对称的，且在分布不重叠时会趋于无穷大，这正是WGAN（Wasserstein GAN）取代基于KL散度的生成对抗网络（GAN）的原因。

questions.question: When would you use Mahalanobis distance instead of Euclidean distance?
questions.questionZh: 在什么情况下应使用马哈拉诺比斯距离（Mahalanobis distance）而非欧几里得距离（Euclidean distance）？
options: When features are all binary (0 or 1)
When features have different scales and are correlated with each other
When you are comparing strings instead of vectors
When you need a faster computation than L2
optionsZh: 当所有特征（features）均为二值（binary）（0 或 1）时
当特征（features）具有不同的尺度（scales）且彼此相关（correlated）时
当您比较字符串 (strings) 而非向量 (vectors) 时
当您需要比 L2 距离（L2 distance）更快的计算时
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis distance accounts for the covariance structure of the data, decorrelating and normalizing features before computing L2 distance. It correctly handles features at different scales and with correlations, whereas Euclidean distance treats all dimensions equally.
questions.explanationZh: 马哈拉诺比斯距离（Mahalanobis distance）考虑了数据的协方差结构（covariance structure），在计算L2距离（L2 distance）之前会对特征进行去相关（decorrelating）与归一化（normalizing）处理。它能够正确处理具有不同量纲和相关性的特征，而欧几里得距离（Euclidean distance）则将所有维度等同对待。
